properties: Ramium fallax, also known as the Autumn Crocus, has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. Its bulbs contain colchicine, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as gout, arthritis, and certain types of cancer. However, it's essential to note that the plant can be toxic and should only be used under medical supervision. Additionally, modern research has shown that colchicine may also have potential applications in wound healing and tissue repair.

Rauvolfia tetraphylla, also known as Ramium, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine due to its health benefits, which include reducing anxiety and stress, and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, such as reserpine, serpentine, and yohimbine. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
